title: How to Make a Wiggly GIF: Step-by-Step Guide | Wiggly Paint
h1: How to Make a Wiggly GIF (Step-by-Step)
description: Learn how to make a wiggly animated GIF right in your browser - no download, no sign-up. A simple step-by-step guide using Wiggly Paint's animated brushes.
excerpt: Turn any doodle into a hand-drawn, wiggling GIF in under a minute - right in your browser, completely free.

paragraphs: A wiggly GIF is a short animation where your drawing appears to shake, jitter or "breathe" - as if it were sketched again and again by hand. The effect feels alive and playful, and you don't need any animation skills to make one. This guide walks you through creating your first wiggly GIF from a blank canvas to a finished, shareable file.

sections.heading: What you need
paragraphs: Nothing to install and no account to create. Wiggly Paint runs entirely in your web browser on desktop, tablet or phone. All you need is something to draw with - a mouse, a trackpad, or your finger on a touchscreen.

sections.heading: Step 1: Open the drawing tool
paragraphs: Open Wiggly Paint and you'll see a blank canvas with a row of brushes. Each brush already includes a built-in wiggle, so the animation happens automatically as you draw - you don't have to set keyframes or a timeline.

sections.heading: Step 2: Pick a wiggly brush
paragraphs: Choose one of the animated brushes. Some give a gentle shiver, others a bouncy, energetic shake. If you're not sure which to use, start with a basic line brush, draw a quick stroke, and watch how it moves. Try a few until you find a wiggle you like.

sections.heading: Step 3: Draw your doodle
paragraphs: Sketch anything - a face, a word, a star, a little creature. Simple shapes read best because the wiggle is doing the work of bringing them to life. Bold outlines and clear shapes animate more clearly than fine, busy detail.
list: Keep lines bold and shapes simple for the clearest motion.
Leave a little empty space around your drawing so the wiggle has room to move.
Combine two or three brushes in one drawing for variety.

sections.heading: Step 4: Export your GIF
paragraphs: When you're happy with the animation, export it as a GIF. The file saves straight to your device, ready to post in a chat, on social media, or anywhere GIFs are supported. There's no watermark and no sign-up step.

sections.heading: Tips for better wiggly GIFs
list: Less is more: a single bold subject usually beats a crowded scene.
Use contrast - a dark drawing on a light canvas (or the reverse) makes the motion pop.
Make a few versions quickly and keep the one that wiggles best.
Short and loopable is the whole point - you don't need a long animation.

sections.heading: That's it
paragraphs: Making a wiggly GIF takes about a minute once you get the hang of it. The best way to learn is to draw a few and see what moves well - so open the canvas and start scribbling.

title: Turn a Drawing Into an Animated GIF - Free, No Download
h1: Turn a Drawing Into an Animated GIF (Free, No Download)
description: Want to animate your sketch? Here's how to turn any drawing into a looping animated GIF online for free, with no software to install and no account required.
excerpt: Got a sketch you love? Here's the easiest way to make it move - online, free, and with nothing to install.

paragraphs: Animating a drawing used to mean expensive software and a steep learning curve. It doesn't anymore. If you can draw a shape, you can turn it into a looping animated GIF in your browser - for free, and without downloading anything. Here's how it works and how to get a good result.

sections.heading: Why a GIF (and not a video)?
paragraphs: GIFs loop forever, play automatically, and work almost everywhere - chats, comments, forums and social posts. For a short animated doodle, a GIF is usually the friendliest format: small, instant, and no play button required.

sections.heading: The fastest way: draw it as an animation
paragraphs: Instead of drawing a still image and animating it afterwards, the quickest route is to draw with a tool where the animation is built in. Wiggly Paint's brushes wiggle as you draw, so the moment you finish a stroke it's already moving. You go from blank canvas to animated GIF without ever touching a timeline.
list: Open the canvas in your browser.
Draw your subject - the wiggle is applied automatically.
Export as a GIF and save it to your device.

sections.heading: Already have a drawing on paper?
paragraphs: If your art lives on paper, the simplest approach is to redraw it on the canvas - it only takes a moment for a simple subject, and you get clean, animation-ready lines. Trace the main shapes rather than copying every detail; bold, simple outlines animate far better than fine pencil work.

sections.heading: Getting a clean, shareable result
list: Keep the subject centered with a little breathing room around it.
Favor a few strong lines over lots of tiny detail.
Pick a wiggle speed that matches the mood - gentle for calm, fast for energetic.
Export and preview; if it feels off, redraw quickly and try again.

sections.heading: Free, private, and instant
paragraphs: Everything happens in your browser. There's no software to install, no account to create, and no watermark on your export. When you're ready, open the canvas and turn your next sketch into something that moves.

title: 10 Fun Things to Draw as Wiggly GIFs (Ideas & Inspiration)
h1: 10 Fun Things to Draw as Wiggly GIFs
description: Stuck for ideas? Here are 10 fun, easy things to draw and animate as wiggly GIFs - perfect for beginners, doodlers and anyone who wants quick inspiration.
excerpt: Blank canvas, blank mind? Here are ten quick, beginner-friendly ideas that look great with a wiggle.

paragraphs: The hardest part of making a wiggly GIF is often just deciding what to draw. The good news: simple, bold subjects wiggle best, so you don't need to be an artist. Here are ten ideas that are easy to draw and look great once they start moving.

sections.heading: 1. A smiley face
paragraphs: The classic starting point. A round face with two dots and a curve becomes surprisingly charming once it jitters - like it's giggling.

sections.heading: 2. Your name or a single word
paragraphs: Hand-lettered text with a wiggle reads like a living sticker. Try "hi", "yay", or your own name in big, bold letters.

sections.heading: 3. A heart
paragraphs: A simple heart outline that pulses and shakes makes a perfect reaction GIF for chats and comments.

sections.heading: 4. A star or sparkle
paragraphs: Stars shimmer naturally when they wiggle. Draw a few different sizes for a twinkling effect.

sections.heading: 5. A little monster or blob creature
paragraphs: Invent a silly creature with a couple of eyes. The wiggle gives it personality without any extra effort - it looks like it's breathing or wobbling along.

sections.heading: 6. A speech bubble
paragraphs: Draw an empty speech bubble or add a short message inside. Animated, it feels like someone is talking.

sections.heading: 7. An arrow
paragraphs: A wiggling arrow is a fun, friendly way to point at something - great for adding emphasis to a post.

sections.heading: 8. Weather doodles
paragraphs: A sun, a cloud, a lightning bolt or a few raindrops all come alive with motion. Combine them for a tiny animated sky.

sections.heading: 9. Food doodles
paragraphs: A slice of pizza, a cupcake, a coffee cup - simple food shapes are instantly recognizable and fun to animate.

sections.heading: 10. An abstract scribble
paragraphs: Sometimes the best result is pure abstraction. Lay down a few loops and squiggles, let them wiggle, and enjoy the hypnotic motion.

sections.heading: Now make one
paragraphs: Pick any idea above, keep it bold and simple, and give it a wiggle. The whole thing takes a minute - and once you make one, it's hard to stop. Open the canvas and try your favorite from the list.
